items.title: Police 'recommence' reporting of child abuse by staff after eight-month gap

children.content: The child safety regulator sought a law change after receiving "concerning" correspondence from Tasmania Police, which said its officers were not bound by a key child protection measure.

gallery.altText: Two police officers stand together - one of them writes on a notepad.

thumbnail.altText: Two police officers stand together - one of them writes on a notepad.

items.title: Residents worry link between minister and wind farm project could dilute advocacy

children.content: Despite the links being declared, some locals worry politicians with a financial stake in a planned development in Tasmania's north will have little sympathy for their concerns about the project.

image.altText: Wind turbines in a field.

items.title: 'Large quantity of arrows' fired in suburbs after archery club theft, police say

children.content: Tasmania Police recover bows and arrows, and arrest and charge two youths over an alleged burglary at a Burnie archery club.

image.altText: A man holds a compound bow in a forest setting.

items.title: Mixed results for tourism sector over Easter amid fuel price surge

children.content: It was a mixed weekend for many tourism businesses across the country, amid fears rising fuel costs would keep people home. For some it was business as usual, but fuel uncertainty has many in the sector feeling nervous about the months ahead.

gallery.altText: Four children sit on a rug in a grassy field, with flowers in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: Four children sit on a rug in a grassy field, with flowers in the foreground.

items.title: Vance calls Trump as US rallies for Viktor Orban

children.content: US President Donald Trump has deployed his vice president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orban, who is flailing in the polls days out from a closely watched election.

image.altText: Viktor Orban and JD Vance wave at a political rally in Hungary

items.title: Having seen prisons from the inside, Zach says they're just 'breeding criminals'

children.content: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

image.altText: A young man stands in front of an Aboriginal flag wearing a grey hoodie with his hands outclasped together in front of his face.

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

gallery.altText: A retired mine pit containing water.
A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

thumbnail.altText: A retired mine pit containing water.
A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

items.title: Where cattle once roamed, conservation now grows — and so do concerns

image.altText: A man leaning on his ute.

children.content: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

items.title: Matt Canavan looks to stars for 'Australia on steroids' plan

children.content: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

image.altText: Matt Canavan, smiling and wearing a suit and tie, walks down a corridor at parliament house.

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

image.altText: computer image of cyclone off queensland coast

items.title: Too much of not enough: Sport stars consider leaving over paltry pay

children.content: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

gallery.altText: Melanie Kawa sits on a bench seat and holds a football.
Melanie Kawa (c) of the Rebels leads the Rebels out to the field.

thumbnail.altText: Melanie Kawa sits on a bench seat and holds a football.
Melanie Kawa (c) of the Rebels leads the Rebels out to the field.

avatar.alt: Profile picture of ABC journalist Kate O'Halloran. She is smiling, wearing a black polo shirt with a white stripe on the collar

items.title: Growing frustration in real estate industry over Qld seller disclosure laws

children.content: Real estate experts say Queensland's seller disclosure laws, which were introduced in August, are fuelling 'gazumping' and leading to unnecessary costs.

image.altText: A red sold sticker across a for sale sign outside a house with a large front yard on a sunny day

items.title: Albanese talks energy security with China's premier

children.content: Anthony Albanese and Chinese Premier Li Qiang have discussed energy security ahead of the prime minister's dash to Singapore later this week. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese meets Li Qiang China

items.title: 'I just had to do it for me': The ambiguous loss of family estrangement

children.content: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

gallery.altText: A man sitting on a rock looking out towards the ocean

thumbnail.altText: A man sitting on a rock looking out towards the ocean

items.title: Quiz: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

image.altText: Elvis Presley impersonators gather for the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of Elvis Presley impersonators.

items.title: Team behind cult 2012 video game reunites on Grammy winner

children.content: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

image.altText: A video game character grinds their board along a large metal chain in what appears to be an underwater ruined city

items.title: Calls to force NDIS providers to register as Labor eyes savings

children.content: Stricter registration rules would weed out low-quality NDIS providers while delivering better services and budget savings, major operators say.

gallery.altText: two adults and a child on a bike in front of a river

thumbnail.altText: two adults and a child on a bike in front of a river

items.title: Boards make moves affecting the nation but look like a golf club committee

children.content: There's a push to get younger people, women and more diverse community members on the boards making the big decisions in Australian life.

image.altText: A smiling young woman stands next to a glass cabinet of historical objects.

items.title: Husband's dying wish for more kids an impossible choice in childcare desert

children.content: In the depths grief, this family wants to grow. But a childcare crisis stands in their way.

image.altText: A woman holds hands with a small boy in front of horses eating hay.

items.title: Tourism industry council warns sector at 'tipping point'

children.content: According to the Queensland Tourism Industry Council (QTIC), many operators are struggling to remain afloat as they grapple with rising operating costs and cancellations, along with the ongoing fuel crisis.

gallery.altText: Two girls looking at Surfers Paradise skyline

thumbnail.altText: Two girls looking at Surfers Paradise skyline

items.title: Court sets aside convictions of parents for starving daughter, grants bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

image.altText: A man and woman walking down the street, covering their heads with jackets

items.title: Darwin midwife's $1.1 million government contract under review

children.content: Darwin midwife Claire Marks was under investigation by the nursing and midwifery watchdog when she was handed a $1.1 million contract by the NT government, which is now reviewing the matter, as doctors continue to raise concerns, and the service brings on new staff.

image.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

items.title: 'Probably lose your hive': Over 1,000 ACT beehives destroyed by varroa mite

children.content: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite, varroa mite, with well over 1,000 destroyed.

gallery.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

thumbnail.altText: A beekeeper lifts a beehive from its structure.

items.title: Passengers vomiting 'up and down' aisles as Fiji flight aborts three landings

children.content: A Fiji Airways flight from Sydney had to divert after bad weather from a tropical cyclone thwarted its attempts to land.

image.altText: A Fiji Airways plane in flight, with a grey cloudy sky in the background.

items.title: 'They blocked menstrual pads': Inside Myanmar's conflict zone, where monasteries are bombed

children.content: Women who had already lost their homes to last year's earthquake are now also cut off from essentials like sanitary pads and aid.

gallery.altText: building on fire, orange flames billowing.

thumbnail.altText: building on fire, orange flames billowing.

items.title: Babies, as young as three months, kidnapped and sold overseas

children.content: Babies, some as young as three months, were allegedly sold to people in Singapore and across Indonesia.

image.altText: Close-up photo of baby feet laying on a white blanket.

items.title: Leg amputee forced to wait two months for rehabilitation services

children.content: After travelling more than 2,000 kilometres across four separate hospitals, Phillip Norris says staff shortages in hospitals "needs to change".

image.altText: A man with an amputated leg sits on a mobility scooter

items.title: Thousands of sites taken down as experts warn AI is increasing scams

children.content: Almost 12,000 scam websites have been shut down by Australia's financial regulator in a year as it continues a "cat-and-mouse game" with cybercriminals.

image.altText: a screenshot of a video of ai generated woman and wallstreet aerial asking viewers to join a waitlist

items.title: David Warner charged with mid-range drink driving

children.content: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner is charged with mid-range drink driving in Sydney's east over the weekend.

image.altText: Cricketer David Warner speaks to media at a press conference, talking and holding his hands up

items.title: Kanye West blocked from entering UK to headline music festival

children.content: The British government's decision forces London's Wireless festival to cancel the event.
